name: George Fassett's Fake Jerky Strips
recipeInstructions:
Mix ground beef with all other ingredients, stirring until well mixed. Place in a food processor with a chopping blade and chop for about a minute until the mixture is smooth and putty-like.
On a floured cutting board, take the ball of meat mixture and coat both sides lightly with flour to prevent sticking. Roll out with a floured rolling pin until it forms an 8-inch by 12-inch rectangle that is no more than 1/8 inch thick.
Using a pizza cutter, cut into 1-inch wide strips that are 8 inches long. Carefully transfer the strips to a wire rack.
Heat oven to 300°F and place the wire rack with meat on the oven rack in the center of the oven, with the second oven rack one notch below it. Place a cookie sheet under the meat on the lower rack to act as a drip pan.
Bake for 1 hour at 300°F.
Mix together sauce ingredients in a shallow dish. Remove the rack and roll each strip into the sauce mix, returning it to the rack. Increase temperature to 450°F and bake for another 15 minutes until the strips are almost burnt around the edges.
Remove the rack again, recoat the strips, and return them to the oven for another 15 minutes or until they become nearly burnt around the edges. Remove from the oven and coat one last time before allowing them to cool to room temperature.
Enjoy!
